---
title: Building raised garden beds
date: 2024-03-02
tags: [gardening, woodworking, diy]
---

I built the beds from rough-sawn cedar because it resists rot without needing chemical treatment. Each bed is four feet wide, which is narrow enough to reach the middle from either side, and twelve feet long so it fits the space between the fence and the path.

The corners are the part most beginners get wrong. Butt joints fastened with deck screws loosen within a few seasons as the wood swells and shrinks. I used simple half-lap joints at the corners and reinforced them with stainless carriage bolts, which has held up for years without a wiggle.

Filling a deep bed cheaply is its own problem. I layered old logs and branches at the bottom, a thick layer of half-finished compost in the middle, and a final six inches of screened topsoil mixed with compost on top. The logs rot slowly and feed the soil for a decade.
